name: mc_knn_standardization_equal_return_opposite_vol_regime
description: AC-1 proof fixture (audit CRITICAL C-1). A 60-day SPY history split into two 30-day regime blocks. The CALM block (days 0-29) has SPY daily returns tightly clustered around today's return (+0.006 decimal, dispersion +/-0.0015) so its rolling 20-day vol is ~0.0015. The CRASH block (days 30-59) has SPY returns widely spread (+0.006 +/- 0.045) so its rolling vol is ~0.045. Today's query point has SPY return == +0.006 (identical to the calm cluster center) AND a genuinely high realized volatility (today_vol ~0.044, a CRASH-regime point because today_vol is computed from the crash-block tail). The holding ticker AAA gains in the calm block (daily_ret +0.020) and loses in the crash block (daily_ret -0.020). An UNSCALED kNN (the pre-fix bug) is return-dominated: it fills its neighbours with calm-block days, the bootstrap distribution is entirely +2.0%, and current_symphony_return (0.0) sits below all of it -> prob_beating snaps to the structural value 100.0. A STANDARDIZED kNN gives the volatility feature a fair share of the distance, correctly recognises today as a crash-regime point, fills its neighbours with crash-block days, the bootstrap distribution is entirely -2.0%, current_symphony_return (0.0) sits above all of it -> prob_beating snaps to the structural value 0.0.
provenance: Schema-derived synthetic history (run_monte_carlo's documented {date:{ticker:{daily_ret}}} contract). The regime selection is observable through prob_beating's terminal value because the holding's per-regime returns have opposite sign. Construction independently verified: see derivation.

calm.meaning: kNN selected calm-regime neighbours (holding gains, bootstrap all +2.0%)
calm.structural_prob_reason: current_symphony_return 0.0 is strictly below every bootstrapped path (+2.0%); searchsorted below_count=0; (paths-0)/paths*100 = 100.0. Not a random MC draw -- a deterministic boundary result.

crash.meaning: kNN selected crash-regime neighbours (holding loses, bootstrap all -2.0%)
crash.structural_prob_reason: current_symphony_return 0.0 is strictly above every bootstrapped path (-2.0%); searchsorted below_count=paths; (paths-paths)/paths*100 = 0.0. Not a random MC draw -- a deterministic boundary result.

derivation: Verified by replicating run_monte_carlo's internal kNN: with the unscaled Euclidean distance sqrt((ret-ret_today)^2 + (vol-vol_today)^2) the 8 nearest days are 100% calm-block days for every k in {5,8,10,15} (calm returns hug today's return, the ~33x-weaker vol feature cannot overcome it). With the standardized distance (z-score both features by window-fitted mean/std) the 8 nearest days are 100% crash-block days. The holding's opposite-sign per-regime returns turn that selection into an observable prob_beating: pre-fix 100.0, post-fix 0.0.
